key_insight: Keeping smoothed-aggregation AMG fully device-resident in a native blocked matrix format removes scalar-format overhead and improves GPU performance for elasticity problems with rectangular block structure.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster and more memory-efficient multigrid solves for large finite-element elasticity systems on multi-GPU A100 platforms, especially when coarse-operator recomputation is repeated across solves.
method: Added a portable Kokkos-backed blocked matrix type in PETSc with rectangular-block kernels.
Kept AMG setup, Galerkin recompute, and V-cycle operations in blocked form without expanding to scalars.
Implemented blocked off-process prolongator gather via PetscSF and blocked COO assembly for dense rectangular blocks.
evidence_signal: On 3D elasticity problems up to 128^3 grid points, the blocked format fit in GPU memory where cuSPARSE did not, and achieved up to 1.24x faster V-cycles, 1.42x faster SpMV, and 2.27x faster coarse-operator recomputation at 64 GPUs.
watch_item: The approach depends on specialized blocked kernels and rectangular block support, so portability and performance may vary across GPU libraries and problem block layouts.
paper_title: A Natively Blocked, Device-Resident Algebraic Multigrid GPU Path in PETSc
